# Resume Activities Ver.2

# School

University of California San Diego  
Field of Study: Computer Science  
Expected Graduate year: 2026

# Skills

C/C++, Python, JavaScript, Dart, SQL, HTML/CSS, Arduino, PyTorch, LangChain, Milvus, Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn, OpenAI API, React, Next.js, Flutter, Vite, Zustand, Material UI, Docker, uv, Git, AWS, Azure, Supabase, Firebase, pytest, Vitest

Python — 毫無疑問放第一  
ROS / ROS2 — 你有基礎，放進去但別誇大  
IsaacLab / MuJoCo — 這是你最稀有的優勢，很多人沒有  
PyTorch — 標配  
Docker — 你有 production 證據  
Reinforcement Learning — reward design、observation/action space 你都做過  
C++ — 你有，robotics 幾乎都要求  
Sim-to-Real — 不只是 buzzword，你在 Triton Droids 和 teleoperation 都在處理這個問題  
URDF / Robot Kinematics — 你在 teleoperation 專案用過  
Computer Vision (CLIP, SAM2, Detectron2) — 你在 UCSD lab 用過

# Rewards

**EMPOWER Scholar | UC San Diego (NSF-Funded)** 

[UCSD EMPOWER Scholars Program](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1FYQn0iXP8cd-JslI0yDZPhUU5LvwwawWW9c3VtH6iC8/edit?usp=sharing)

**Dates:** 2024 – 2026

**Funding:** $20,000 Total ($10k/year)

**Core Competencies & Keywords:**

* National Science Foundation (NSF) S-STEM Program  
* Jacobs School of Engineering  
* Technical & Professional Development  
* Engineering Resilience & Retention  
* Research Preparation

**Detailed Bullet Points (Mix & Match for Resumes):**

* **Selection:** Chosen for a highly competitive NSF-sponsored program supporting high-potential engineering transfer students; awarded $20,000 over two years to support academic and research endeavors.  
* **Development:** Completed a 4-week Summer Internship Prep Program focused on technical development, professional networking, and soft-skill refinement.  
* **Mentorship:** Actively collaborated with engineering faculty mentors and industry alumni to refine career goals and gain exposure to graduate-level research opportunities.  
* **Research Potential:** Eligible for an additional $5,000 fellowship to conduct faculty-mentored summer research and present findings at the UCSD Undergraduate Research Conference. *(Note: Keep this bullet only if you plan to utilize the summer research portion).*  
* **Soft Skills:** demonstrated "enthusiasm, persistence, and resilience" (official selection criteria) in navigating complex engineering curriculum.

**Alan Turing Memorial Scholar | Center for Networked Systems (CNS)** 

[Essay for All Scholarship](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1nCTuOmV1VR_nw30UHQCnhQNab-y-PwohqUqNQy1BVOo/edit?usp=sharing)

**Dates:** 2025 – 2026 

**Funding:** $12,000 Total ($10,000 Memorial Scholarship \+ $2,000 Teradata Scholarship)

**Core Competencies & Keywords:**

* Networked Systems & Computer Engineering  
* Diversity, Equity, & Inclusion (DEI)  
* Center for Networked Systems (CNS)  
* Research & Mentorship

**Detailed Bullet Points (Mix & Match):**

* **Selection:** Awarded a highly competitive endowed scholarship established to honor Alan Turing; selected based on academic merit in networked systems and active support for inclusivity in engineering.  
* **Funding:** Recipient of $12,000 in total funding, combining the Alan Turing Memorial Scholarship and the corporate-sponsored Teradata Scholarship.  
* **Affiliation:** Directly affiliated with the Center for Networked Systems (CNS), a research unit within the UC San Diego Computer Science and Engineering department.  
* **DEI Leadership:** Recognized for supporting the LGBTQIA+ community in STEM, honoring the legacy of Alan Turing. *\[Use this bullet only if you are applying to companies with strong DEI values like Google, Apple, or Salesforce\].*  
* **Research:** Eligible for faculty-guided research opportunities within the CNS infrastructure, focusing on \[insert specific network/CS topics if applicable\].

# Experience

**Software Engineering Intern** | June 25, 2025 \- September 12, 2025  
Ubitus K.K. | Taiwan, Taipei

- Learned and implemented RAG, VectorDB, and LangGraph to develop a MCP server for an LLM to perform searching on the Milvus vector database.  
- Transform the company’s existing database search algorithm to a MCP server.  
- Improve the search algorithm to enable cross multiple collection searching.  
- Engineered a search MCP server for an LLM to query a Milvus vector database, enabling cross-collection searching and improving final output accuracy to a 77.8% F1 Score by implementing a re-ranking model for superior relevance over standard L2 distance filtering.  
- Architected a prompt engineering defense framework that mitigated 100% of injection attacks across five AI applications by designing resilient system prompts with unchangeable AI identity.  
- Served as the primary technical operator for the G1 humanoid robot at the AI WAVE exhibition, leading live interactive demonstrations and collecting critical user interaction data designated for the development of future reinforcement learning models.

Engineered a High-Performance AI system by developing a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that enables the company's LLM to perform scalable cross-collection semantic searches on a Milvus vector database. I implemented re-ranking models and architecture optimization to achieve a 77.8% F1 Score and improve search performance, then containerized the solution using Docker for scalable inference/deployment. Furthermore, I architected and deployed a company-wide Agentic AI orchestration framework for prompt injection defense, achieving a 100% mitigation rate across five AI applications. I also served as primary technical operator for the G1 humanoid robot at the AI WAVE exhibition, collecting critical user interaction data designated for future reinforcement learning models.

Extended utkusen/promptmap (open-source LLM injection scanner) with an Improver LLM that reads failed attack tests and auto-rewrites vulnerable system prompts, closing the test-to-fix loop. Refactored the single-file tool into a modular Python package, added UbiGPT provider support and an override rule category; applied it to iteratively harden system prompts across 5 internal LLM products.

**Software Engineering Intern (AI Team)** | January 6, 2025 \- May 7, 2025  
Swing Phi | Remote

- Engineered a political data analysis model by integrating 7 different APIs and government databases, enabling  
- Real-time tracking of legislative actions’ impact on market movements.  
- Developed an automated data aggregation system by connecting to Senate Lobby Disclosure database and  
- Financial Disclosure portals, streamlining the monitoring of political trading activities.  
- Created a comprehensive tracking solution by implementing real-time news and social media analysis, producing  
- Actionable insights on legislative influence on financial markets

Engineered a comprehensive political data analysis AI system by integrating 7 APIs and government databases (Senate Lobby Disclosure, Financial Disclosure portals) to track real-time correlations between legislative actions and financial market movements. I developed an automated data aggregation system using web scraping (Selenium, BeautifulSoup) to gather crucial government data, and utilized the Azure OpenAI ChatGPT-4 API to provide actionable insights on political trading patterns and their market implications.

**Computer Vision Researcher** | September 2024 \- May 2025  
UCSD | San Diego, CA

- Contributed to making SceneProgLLM better by adding advanced APIs (i.e., Anthropic, Ollama) and improving supporting local images for scene rendering pipelines  
- Worked with team members in designing domain-specific languages (DSLs) for scene synthesis in providing structured interactivity for LLM and 3D databases like 3DFront  
- Designed and implemented prompts to evaluate the impact of different ceiling light positions on 3D scene illumination, leveraging LLMs to generate scored image outputs with detailed reasoning across various quality metrics.  
- Key Technologies & Tools: Python, Blender, LangChain, CLIP, SAM2, Detectron2, LLaMA, OpenAI APIs, Docker, GitHub.  
- 

Contributed to Foundational Model research by integrating LLMs and advanced APIs (Anthropic, Ollama, OpenAI) and implementing 3D scene rendering pipelines for structured LLM-3D database interaction. I designed and implemented computer vision evaluation pipelines using tools like CLIP, SAM2, and Detectron2 for 3D scene optimization/performance analysis. This work involved leveraging LLMs as black-box optimizers for complex spatial reasoning tasks, such as lighting placement and depth estimation.

**Robotics Instructor** |  July 2024 \- September 2024  
Magikid Robotics Lab | Fremont, CA

- Instructed robotics students in assembling OLLO and VEX GO robots.  
- Taught coding fundamentals and created games through Scratch.  
- Guided students in creating 3D models using TinkerCAD and handled the 3D printing process.  
- Introduced students to various AI technologies, including ChatGPT, Suno, and AI Image Generators, to keep them abreast of advancing technologies

![][image1]  
As a Robotics Instructor at Magikid Robotics Lab from July 2024 to September 2024, I guided students in hands-on learning experiences that combined robotics, coding, and emerging technologies. I taught students how to assemble OLLO and VEX GO robots, fostering their understanding of mechanical systems and engineering principles. Additionally, I introduced them to coding fundamentals by creating interactive games using Scratch, helping them develop logical thinking and problem-solving skills. To expand their creativity, I instructed students in designing 3D models with TinkerCAD and managed the 3D printing process, enabling them to bring their digital designs to life. Beyond robotics and coding, I exposed students to cutting-edge AI technologies such as ChatGPT, Suno, and AI Image Generators, ensuring they stayed informed about advancements in artificial intelligence. This role allowed me to combine technical instruction with mentorship, inspiring students to explore the intersection of technology and innovation.

**Software Developer Intern** | June 2023 \- August 2023  
Frugal Innovation Hub. | Santa Clara, CA

- Develop an App that offers a seamless and user-friendly interface, allowing users to effortlessly select their desired food items from an extensive menu.  
- Overcoming language obstacles, the app enables users to input their order in their native language. OrderEase employs advanced translation technology to generate corresponding English phrases that users can read off to the store clerks confidently.

I worked as an intern on the Order Simulator project at Santa Clara University's Frugal Innovation Hub, developing a mobile application to bridge language barriers in food ordering. Using Flutter for development and Figma for design, we created a user-friendly interface that allows users to browse menu items and place orders in their native language. The app then generates clear English phrases that users can confidently communicate to store clerks. Working alongside my teammate under the guidance of advisor Silvia Figueira, I gained valuable experience in both technical development and soft skills. The project required full-stack development expertise, from creating intuitive frontend interfaces to implementing robust backend translation functionality. This humanitarian-focused project, supported by NSF and other organizations, aims to empower individuals worldwide by reducing anxiety and unfamiliarity when ordering food in English-speaking environments.

Worked as an intern on the Order Simulator project at Santa Clara University’s Frugal Innovation Hub, developing a Flutter mobile app to help users overcome language barriers in food ordering. Designed intuitive interfaces in Figma and implemented frontend and backend features that translate native-language selections into clear English phrases for real-world use. Collaborated with a teammate under advisor Silvia Figueira on this NSF-supported project, gaining experience in full-stack development and building user-centered solutions with real social impact.

**Lab Research Intern** | January 2023 \- April 2023  
BioChain Institute Inc. | Newark, CA

- Organized over 300 items to facilitate inventory and streamline sales.  
- Scanned over 1000 slides using Aperio Lv1 for customer demos.  
- Sought customer feedback from users of the company products.  
- Mentored fellow interns on the scanning process and data organization.

During my internship at BioChain Institute Inc., I contributed to improving operational efficiency by organizing over 300 inventory items. This effort streamlined the company’s inventory management process and facilitated smoother sales operations. By ensuring items were properly categorized and accessible, I helped optimize workflows and supported the team in meeting customer demands more effectively.

A key aspect of my role involved scanning over 1,000 slides using the Aperio Lv1 scanner for customer demonstrations. This task required precision and attention to detail to ensure the slides were accurately prepared and presented. These demonstrations played a crucial role in showcasing the company’s products to clients, enhancing their understanding of the tools’ capabilities.

In addition to my technical responsibilities, I actively sought feedback from customers who used the company’s products. By gathering insights directly from users, I helped the team identify areas for improvement and align product offerings with customer needs.

Furthermore, I took on a mentorship role by guiding fellow interns in mastering the scanning process and organizing data efficiently. This not only ensured consistency in our work but also fostered a collaborative learning environment within the team. My contributions during this internship demonstrated my ability to balance technical tasks with leadership and organizational skills.

# Clubs

**Triton Droids, UC San Diego** — ML Team Member (February 2026 – present)

We're building a humanoid robot from the ground up. The hardware team handles mechanical and electrical. I'm on ML, working alongside the simulation and RL people.

My two projects right now: a falling policy in MuJoCo so the robot goes down safely and cleanly when it loses balance, using imitation learning and RL. And a Panda arm pick-and-place pipeline, also in simulation.

What makes this different from most school projects is that the hardware team is building the physical robot at the same time we're writing the policies for it. The sim-to-real gap isn't a case study. It's showing up in every meeting.

**Chinese Engineering Society, UC San Diego** — Career Development Program Team Officer (November 2024 – present)

Host various events for students to know 

# Projects

**Humanoid Robot Teleoperation** | Python, IsaacLab, MuJoCo, OpenSim, MediaPipe, FastAPI, ESP32, Jetson, Holosoma | Jan. 2026 – Present  
Self / UC San Diego Team | San Diego, CA

- Building a real-time humanoid teleoperation system that maps human lower-body motion to robot leg commands using wearable IMU sensors and camera-based pose estimation.  
- Developing motion-tracking RL policies in IsaacLab to imitate target leg key points while maintaining balance, with custom reward design, PD-control action spaces, and robot-state observations.  
- Evaluating policy robustness in MuJoCo prior to hardware deployment to support smoother sim-to-real transfer for humanoid locomotion.  
- Working with a multidisciplinary team on pose construction, retargeting, and deployment pipelines from sensing and URDF alignment through simulation and real-robot execution.

Building a real-time lower-body teleoperation system for humanoid robots using wearable IMUs, pose retargeting, reinforcement learning in IsaacLab, and sim-to-real validation in MuJoCo.

**Love Ability Gym** | React.js (v19), Vite, Supabase, Zustand, Framer Motion, Recharts, i18next, PWA, Vitest / React Testing Library | Jan. 2026 \- Present

Self | San Diego, CA

- Built a bilingual progressive web app to help users improve emotional awareness and relationship skills by combining structured self-reflection flows with offline-capable PWA support.  
- Reduced one-size-fits-all guidance by designing state-aware product flows across Self State Check, Relationship X-Ray, and Projection Check modules that adapt exercises to the user’s current emotional condition.  
- Turned multiple psychological frameworks into actionable feedback by developing scoring and mapping logic for DISC, Core Values, Attachment, and Happiness assessments.  
- Enabled longitudinal reflection and personalized next-step guidance by integrating Zustand and Supabase for assessment history, retake logic, cross-session continuity, and progress tracking.  
- Improved reliability and user safety by implementing crisis-access flows, sensitive edge-case validation, and frontend test coverage for core scoring and user-flow logic.

**NewLedger** | Swift, Flutter | ( hours) | Dec. 2024 \- Present  
Self | Fremont, CA

- For Technical Implementation:  
  - Engineered a cross-platform financial management application using Flutter and Firebase, implementing real-time expense tracking and analytics for seamless synchronization across iOS and Android devices  
  - Integrated ML Kit for receipt scanning functionality by developing custom OCR algorithms, enabling automated expense entry and reducing manual data input time by 70%  
  - Architected a scalable database structure using Firebase and Hive, implementing secure user authentication and data persistence for handling complex expense splitting calculations across multiple users

- For Feature Development:  
  - Developed an intuitive expense splitting system by implementing collaborative features and real-time updates, enabling efficient cost sharing among multiple users within friend groups  
  - Created comprehensive financial analytics dashboard using Material Design, providing users with detailed spending insights through interactive charts and customizable reports  
  - Implemented multi-currency support and automated currency conversion by integrating exchange rate APIs, allowing users to track expenses across different currencies seamlessly

- For User Experience:  
  - Designed and implemented dark/light theme functionality using Provider state management, enhancing user experience with customizable interface options  
  - Built an advanced filtering system for expense categorization and search, enabling users to efficiently manage and analyze their financial data  
  - Incorporated robust data visualization features using custom Flutter widgets, presenting complex financial data through intuitive and interactive charts

I started to build this app from the embarrassment of not being able to answer the question, "How much money did you spend in total for this five day trip?" There are many expenses throughout the trip but I didn't pay attention to memorizing all of them. To not experience this embarrassment ever again, I want to have an app that helps me memorize everything and when others ask me the similar question again, I can simply pull this app out and open the specific categories for total expenses of those categories and individual small expenses. The modern expense tracking app doesn't seem to have this function or they cost money to use this function, so I thought of creating one that perfectly matches my desire. Starting off, the app should be able to add the expenses and categorize them in the user desired categories. On top of this, I tried to make the adding expense process easier so I implemented the camera scanning function and autofill the information for the user with the image of receipt, and also be able to save this receipt for future reference on detailed transactions. After adding the detailed information of the expense, the user would want to decide which category they want to put this expense in. The user can either choose from default categories or create their own categories. After the main functionality of this app is completed, I thought it'd be great to have a collaborator system in my app just for convenience and also record who was involved during the event/category.

At first I developed these using Swift since I’m an Iphone user and thought of using the app myself, however, after I share my app with my family and friends, they showed interested in it and because many of them are Android user, I could only switch to Dart for providing the app for both system users. The process of transitioning from Swift to Dart was tough because Swift has many libraries for me to use directly and they all look really pretty with a good UI, but with Dart, I had to create mostly everything myself without using MaterialUI.

Throughout the development process, I used ClaudeAI the most to help me debug and understand some of the syntax. Moreover, I also asked it to teach me how to store the information locally. I’ve been using firebase to store things so it was my first time trying the provider. Sometimes I ask it to also improve my UI for convenience.

**Flashy** | NextJS, JavaScript, Firebase, GroqAI, Clerk, Stripe, Material UI | (60 hours) | August 2024  
Headstarter | Fremont, CA

- Designed and launched Flashy, an AI-driven flashcard creation platform that generates up to 12 flashcards per session, promoting critical thinking and memory retention with thought-provoking questions and detailed answers including examples and context.  
- Built a secure, scalable backend with Firebase, enabling users to create, save, and edit an unlimited number of flashcards, and integrated Clerk's authentication to safeguard data for 10+ active users with zero data breaches.  
- Optimized user experience with a highly intuitive interface, resulting in a 40% increase in user retention and empowering students and professionals to customize flashcards for effective learning and skill mastery.  
- [https://flashyai.vercel.app/](https://flashyai.vercel.app/)

**Hosted a Static Website on Amazon S3** | AWS July 2024  
Self-Learning | Fremont, CA

**Arduino Joystick Control Car** | Ohlone Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers January 2023 \- April 2023  
IEEE Club Vice President | Arduino

- Printed a 3D model race car that incorporated light sensors, a microprocessor, and an Arduino board.  
- Wrote code to implement design.  
- Showcased the autonomous race car in Club Rush and Career Fair at Ohlone College.

As Vice President of a club at Ohlone College, I successfully led a team in designing and building a joystick-controlled car using Arduino and C++ programming. My responsibilities included teaching members essential skills such as 3D printing, 3D model design using Fusion360, proper wiring assembly, soldering techniques, and integrating hardware with software. I also guided the team in establishing communication between the joystick controller and the receiver to ensure seamless functionality.

One of the key challenges I faced was managing task assignments and effectively teaching a large group within a limited timeframe. To address this, I implemented a structured approach by appointing team leaders, dividing tasks into manageable segments, and organizing meetings of varying types and scales to track progress efficiently. For technical challenges, I encouraged members to identify issues during meetings and facilitated collaboration by matching those with expertise to specific problems.

Through this collaborative and organized effort, we successfully built the joystick-controlled car and showcased it at an on-campus club and career fair. This experience not only demonstrated my technical knowledge but also highlighted my leadership, problem-solving, and team management skills.

# Resume Activities

**School**  
University of California San Diego  
Field of Study: Computer Science

**Experience**

**Software Engineering Intern** | June 25, 2025 \- September 12, 2025  
Ubitus K.K. | Taiwan, Taipei

- Learned and implemented RAG, VectorDB, and LangGraph to develop a MCP server for an LLM to perform searching on the Milvus vector database.  
- Transform the company’s existing database search algorithm to a MCP server.  
- Improve the search algorithm to enable cross multiple collection searching.  
- Engineered a search MCP server for an LLM to query a Milvus vector database, enabling cross-collection searching and improving final output accuracy to a 77.8% F1 Score by implementing a re-ranking model for superior relevance over standard L2 distance filtering.  
- Architected a prompt engineering defense framework that mitigated 100% of injection attacks across five AI applications by designing resilient system prompts with unchangeable AI identity.  
- Served as the primary technical operator for the G1 humanoid robot at the AI WAVE exhibition, leading live interactive demonstrations and collecting critical user interaction data designated for the development of future reinforcement learning models.

I developed advanced AI infrastructure solutions at Ubitus K.K., where I engineered a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server enabling the company’s LLM to perform cross-collection semantic searches on a Milvus vector database. By implementing sophisticated re-ranking models and optimizing the architecture from a Three Tools to One Tool approach, I achieved a 77.8% F1 Score while improving search performance by 95%. I also architected a comprehensive prompt injection defense framework with 63 test rules across 7 attack categories, achieving 100% mitigation rate against injection attacks across five AI applications. Additionally, I served as the primary technical operator for the G1 humanoid robot at the AI WAVE exhibition, leading live demonstrations and collecting user interaction data for future reinforcement learning model development. This experience taught me that exceptional engineering requires not just technical implementation, but systematic problem-solving and holistic understanding of complex systems.

**Software Engineering Intern (AI Team)** | January 6, 2025 \- May 7, 2025  
Swing Phi | Remote

- Engineered a political data analysis model by integrating 7 different APIs and government databases, enabling  
- Real-time tracking of legislative actions’ impact on market movements.  
- Developed an automated data aggregation system by connecting to Senate Lobby Disclosure database and  
- Financial Disclosure portals, streamlining the monitoring of political trading activities.  
- Created a comprehensive tracking solution by implementing real-time news and social media analysis, producing  
- Actionable insights on legislative influence on financial markets

I developed a comprehensive political data analysis model that integrates multiple government databases and APIs to track how legislative actions impact financial markets. The system combines data from Senate Lobby Disclosure databases, Financial Disclosure portals, and real-time news sources. By January 2025, I implemented web scraping solutions using Selenium and BeautifulSoup to gather data from Senate Financial Disclosures, the Office of Government Ethics, and social media platforms when direct APIs weren't available.

The system analyzes correlations between political activities and market movements, tracking detailed information like disclosure dates, transaction details, and asset information. Using Azure OpenAI's ChatGPT-4 API, the model provides insights into why specific political figures make certain stock purchases and how their legislative actions relate to market changes. The project integrates with existing news analysis systems to provide a complete picture of political trading patterns and their market implications.

**Computer Vision Research** | September 2024 \- May 2025  
UCSD | San Diego, CA

- Contributed to making SceneProgLLM better by adding advanced APIs (i.e., Anthropic, Ollama) and improving supporting local images for scene rendering pipelines  
- Worked with team members in designing domain-specific languages (DSLs) for scene synthesis in providing structured interactivity for LLM and 3D databases like 3DFront  
- Designed and implemented prompts to evaluate the impact of different ceiling light positions on 3D scene illumination, leveraging LLMs to generate scored image outputs with detailed reasoning across various quality metrics.  
- Key Technologies & Tools: Python, Blender, LangChain, CLIP, SAM2, Detectron2, LLaMA, OpenAI APIs, Docker, GitHub.  
- 

During computer vision research at UCSD with Professor Manmohan Chandraker, I contributed to developing innovative methodologies for 3D scene understanding, evaluation, and synthesis by integrating large language models (LLMs) and vision-language models (VLMs) with two other teammates. Our work focused on leveraging these tools to address spatial reasoning challenges and optimize 3D scene layouts. Specifically, we implemented functions in Blender for light placement optimization and designed prompts to evaluate lighting conditions using VLMs as black-box optimizers. We explored heuristic-based approaches, such as greedy XY methods, and reinforcement learning techniques like REINFORCE for non-differentiable optimization tasks. Additionally, we worked on creating pipelines that combined domain-specific languages (DSLs) with LLM-driven scene rendering frameworks, such as SceneProg and SceneCraft, to automate camera placement heuristics and enhance object-centric scene visualization.

Our research also involved designing strategies to communicate global and local frames of reference to LLMs for tasks like object localization and lighting direction analysis. We tested these approaches using permutations of simple scenes to evaluate the models' ability to interpret spatial relationships. Furthermore, we integrated computer vision tools like SAM2, Detectron2, and CLIP into a visual evaluator pipeline to improve semantic filtering and depth estimation for scene evaluation. This work included experimenting with structured outputs under varying conditions and exploring the trade-offs between engineered DSLs and LLM-designed DSLs.

Through this project, I gained hands-on experience with advanced tools such as Blender, LangChain, and various computer vision frameworks while honing my skills in interdisciplinary problem-solving and collaborative research. My contributions were pivotal in advancing the use of AI for 3D scene synthesis and evaluation, providing a foundation for further exploration in spatial reasoning tasks. This research not only deepened my technical expertise but also prepared me to tackle complex challenges at the intersection of AI, computer vision, and 3D graphics.

  - Personal branding and networking  
  - Interview preparation and presentation skills  
  - Teamwork and collaboration  
  - Meeting deadlines and delivering under pressure  
  - Giving and receiving constructive feedback  
  - Pitching and selling ideas effectively  
- Gained hands-on experience in translating theoretical knowledge into practical solutions  
- Collaborated with 3 peers and mentors to solve complex software engineering challenges  
- Enhanced my technical abilities in AI and software development while also strengthening my professional skill set.

During my intensive 7-week Software Engineering fellowship at Headstarter, I demonstrated strong technical capabilities by developing four significant AI-driven applications. My flagship project, Flashy, is an innovative AI-powered flashcard creation platform built with NextJS, Firebase, and GroqAI. This platform generates up to 12 detailed flashcards per session, incorporating thought-provoking questions and comprehensive answers. The implementation of Firebase backend and Clerk authentication ensured secure data management for over 10 active users, while the intuitive interface led to a 40% increase in user retention.

I also developed ListVaults, a comprehensive watch list management platform that serves 20+ users, allowing them to organize and track various media content. The platform's seamless functionality for moving items between to-watch and watched sections resulted in a 14% increase in user engagement. Additionally, I created PlanMyTrip, an AI-powered travel planning platform using Groq AI and ReactJS, which streamlines the travel planning process through personalized itineraries and interactive features. To showcase these projects, I designed and deployed a personal landing page using HTML and CSS, creating a centralized portfolio of my work.

Throughout these projects, I demonstrated proficiency in modern web technologies including NextJS, JavaScript, Firebase, and various AI integrations, while maintaining a strong focus on user experience and practical functionality. Each project addressed specific user needs and incorporated measurable improvements in user engagement and satisfaction, reflecting my ability to deliver impactful solutions within the fellowship's intensive timeline.

**Robotics Instructor** |  July 2024 \- September 2024  
Magikid Robotics Lab | Fremont, CA

- Instructed robotics students in assembling OLLO and VEX GO robots.  
- Taught coding fundamentals and created games through Scratch.  
- Guided students in creating 3D models using TinkerCAD and handled the 3D printing process.  
- Introduced students to various AI technologies, including ChatGPT, Suno, and AI Image Generators, to keep them abreast of advancing technologies

![][image1]  
As a Robotics Instructor at Magikid Robotics Lab from July 2024 to September 2024, I guided students in hands-on learning experiences that combined robotics, coding, and emerging technologies. I taught students how to assemble OLLO and VEX GO robots, fostering their understanding of mechanical systems and engineering principles. Additionally, I introduced them to coding fundamentals by creating interactive games using Scratch, helping them develop logical thinking and problem-solving skills. To expand their creativity, I instructed students in designing 3D models with TinkerCAD and managed the 3D printing process, enabling them to bring their digital designs to life. Beyond robotics and coding, I exposed students to cutting-edge AI technologies such as ChatGPT, Suno, and AI Image Generators, ensuring they stayed informed about advancements in artificial intelligence. This role allowed me to combine technical instruction with mentorship, inspiring students to explore the intersection of technology and innovation.

**Software Developer Intern** | June 2023 \- August 2023  
Frugal Innovation Hub. | Santa Clara, CA

- Develop an App that offers a seamless and user-friendly interface, allowing users to effortlessly select their desired food items from an extensive menu.  
- Overcoming language obstacles, the app enables users to input their order in their native language. OrderEase employs advanced translation technology to generate corresponding English phrases that users can read off to the store clerks confidently.

I worked as an intern on the Order Simulator project at Santa Clara University's Frugal Innovation Hub, developing a mobile application to bridge language barriers in food ordering. Using Flutter for development and Figma for design, we created a user-friendly interface that allows users to browse menu items and place orders in their native language. The app then generates clear English phrases that users can confidently communicate to store clerks. Working alongside my teammate under the guidance of advisor Silvia Figueira, I gained valuable experience in both technical development and soft skills. The project required full-stack development expertise, from creating intuitive frontend interfaces to implementing robust backend translation functionality. This humanitarian-focused project, supported by NSF and other organizations, aims to empower individuals worldwide by reducing anxiety and unfamiliarity when ordering food in English-speaking environments.

**Lab Research Intern** | January 2023 \- April 2023  
BioChain Institute Inc. | Newark, CA

- Organized over 300 items to facilitate inventory and streamline sales.  
- Scanned over 1000 slides using Aperio Lv1 for customer demos.  
- Sought customer feedback from users of the company products.  
- Mentored fellow interns on the scanning process and data organization.

During my internship at BioChain Institute Inc., I contributed to improving operational efficiency by organizing over 300 inventory items. This effort streamlined the company’s inventory management process and facilitated smoother sales operations. By ensuring items were properly categorized and accessible, I helped optimize workflows and supported the team in meeting customer demands more effectively.

A key aspect of my role involved scanning over 1,000 slides using the Aperio Lv1 scanner for customer demonstrations. This task required precision and attention to detail to ensure the slides were accurately prepared and presented. These demonstrations played a crucial role in showcasing the company’s products to clients, enhancing their understanding of the tools’ capabilities.

In addition to my technical responsibilities, I actively sought feedback from customers who used the company’s products. By gathering insights directly from users, I helped the team identify areas for improvement and align product offerings with customer needs.

Furthermore, I took on a mentorship role by guiding fellow interns in mastering the scanning process and organizing data efficiently. This not only ensured consistency in our work but also fostered a collaborative learning environment within the team. My contributions during this internship demonstrated my ability to balance technical tasks with leadership and organizational skills.

**Projects**

**NewLedger** | Swift, Flutter | ( hours) | Dec. 2024 \- Present  
Self | Fremont, CA

- For Technical Implementation:  
  - Engineered a cross-platform financial management application using Flutter and Firebase, implementing real-time expense tracking and analytics for seamless synchronization across iOS and Android devices  
  - Integrated ML Kit for receipt scanning functionality by developing custom OCR algorithms, enabling automated expense entry and reducing manual data input time by 70%  
  - Architected a scalable database structure using Firebase and Hive, implementing secure user authentication and data persistence for handling complex expense splitting calculations across multiple users

- For Feature Development:  
  - Developed an intuitive expense splitting system by implementing collaborative features and real-time updates, enabling efficient cost sharing among multiple users within friend groups  
  - Created comprehensive financial analytics dashboard using Material Design, providing users with detailed spending insights through interactive charts and customizable reports  
  - Implemented multi-currency support and automated currency conversion by integrating exchange rate APIs, allowing users to track expenses across different currencies seamlessly

- For User Experience:  
  - Designed and implemented dark/light theme functionality using Provider state management, enhancing user experience with customizable interface options  
  - Built an advanced filtering system for expense categorization and search, enabling users to efficiently manage and analyze their financial data  
  - Incorporated robust data visualization features using custom Flutter widgets, presenting complex financial data through intuitive and interactive charts

I started to build this app from the embarrassment of not being able to answer the question, "How much money did you spend in total for this five day trip?" There are many expenses throughout the trip but I didn't pay attention to memorizing all of them. To not experience this embarrassment ever again, I want to have an app that helps me memorize everything and when others ask me the similar question again, I can simply pull this app out and open the specific categories for total expenses of those categories and individual small expenses. The modern expense tracking app doesn't seem to have this function or they cost money to use this function, so I thought of creating one that perfectly matches my desire. Starting off, the app should be able to add the expenses and categorize them in the user desired categories. On top of this, I tried to make the adding expense process easier so I implemented the camera scanning function and autofill the information for the user with the image of receipt, and also be able to save this receipt for future reference on detailed transactions. After adding the detailed information of the expense, the user would want to decide which category they want to put this expense in. The user can either choose from default categories or create their own categories. After the main functionality of this app is completed, I thought it'd be great to have a collaborator system in my app just for convenience and also record who was involved during the event/category.

At first I developed these using Swift since I’m an Iphone user and thought of using the app myself, however, after I share my app with my family and friends, they showed interested in it and because many of them are Android user, I could only switch to Dart for providing the app for both system users. The process of transitioning from Swift to Dart was tough because Swift has many libraries for me to use directly and they all look really pretty with a good UI, but with Dart, I had to create mostly everything myself without using MaterialUI.

Throughout the development process, I used ClaudeAI the most to help me debug and understand some of the syntax. Moreover, I also asked it to teach me how to store the information locally. I’ve been using firebase to store things so it was my first time trying the provider. Sometimes I ask it to also improve my UI for convenience.

**Flashy** | NextJS, JavaScript, Firebase, GroqAI, Clerk, Stripe, Material UI | (60 hours) | August 2024  
Headstarter | Fremont, CA

- Designed and launched Flashy, an AI-driven flashcard creation platform that generates up to 12 flashcards per session, promoting critical thinking and memory retention with thought-provoking questions and detailed answers including examples and context.  
- Built a secure, scalable backend with Firebase, enabling users to create, save, and edit an unlimited number of flashcards, and integrated Clerk's authentication to safeguard data for 10+ active users with zero data breaches.  
- Optimized user experience with a highly intuitive interface, resulting in a 40% increase in user retention and empowering students and professionals to customize flashcards for effective learning and skill mastery.  
- [https://flashyai.vercel.app/](https://flashyai.vercel.app/)

![][image2]

**ListVaults** | NextJS, JavaScript, Firebase | (40 hours) | August 2024  
Headstarter | Fremont, CA

- Deployed a comprehensive website on Vercel, attracting 20+ users to manage and track various watch lists, including anime and drama.  
- Enabled the creation of different types of lists on a single platform with dedicated "to-watch" and "watched" sections, improving user organization by 30\\%.  
- Implemented seamless item movement from "to-watch" to "watched" upon completion, increasing user engagement by 14\\%.  
- Provided functionality for users to edit list items, enhancing customization options and user satisfaction by 20\\%.  
- [https://listvaults.vercel.app/](https://listvaults.vercel.app/)

![][image3]

**PlanMyTrip** | Groq AI, ReactJS, NextJS, Material UI | (40 hours) | August 2024  
Headstarter | Fremont, CA

- Developed PlanMyTrip, an AI-powered platform designed to simplify travel planning and enhance the user experience.  
- Created a seamless and interactive interface that offers tailored travel recommendations and personalized itinerary mapping.  
- Integrated packing checklists, essential travel tips, and timely reminders for key tasks like booking accommodations, arranging transportation, and document preparation.  
- Streamlined complex logistics to allow users—whether solo travelers or groups—to focus on enjoying their journey, rather than managing details.  
- Adapted the platform to meet unique user preferences and optimize travel preparation for varied travel styles and schedules.  
- [https://plan-my-trip-ai.vercel.app/](https://plan-my-trip-ai.vercel.app/)

**Personal Landing Page** | HTML, CSS, DNS, Google Analytics | July 2024  
Headstarter | Fremont, CA

- Created a personal landing page to analog all of my projects  
- [https://yanglin14.github.io/personal-website/](https://yanglin14.github.io/personal-website/)

**Hosted a Static Website on Amazon S3** | AWS July 2024  
Self-Learning | Fremont, CA

- Created and hosted a personalized, publicly accessible static website on an S3 bucket by uploading HTML and image files, enabling public access, and implementing a bucket policy to prevent file deletion and versioning to recover previous file versions.  
- Purchased a domain on Route 53 to host the website with a unique URL, and utilized pre-signed URLs to temporarily share objects in the bucket.

**Arduino Joystick Control Car** | Ohlone Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers January 2023 \- April 2023  
IEEE Club Vice President | Arduino

- Printed a 3D model race car that incorporated light sensors, a microprocessor, and an Arduino board.  
- Wrote code to implement design.  
- Showcased the autonomous race car in Club Rush and Career Fair at Ohlone College.

As Vice President of a club at Ohlone College, I successfully led a team in designing and building a joystick-controlled car using Arduino and C++ programming. My responsibilities included teaching members essential skills such as 3D printing, 3D model design using Fusion360, proper wiring assembly, soldering techniques, and integrating hardware with software. I also guided the team in establishing communication between the joystick controller and the receiver to ensure seamless functionality.

One of the key challenges I faced was managing task assignments and effectively teaching a large group within a limited timeframe. To address this, I implemented a structured approach by appointing team leaders, dividing tasks into manageable segments, and organizing meetings of varying types and scales to track progress efficiently. For technical challenges, I encouraged members to identify issues during meetings and facilitated collaboration by matching those with expertise to specific problems.

Through this collaborative and organized effort, we successfully built the joystick-controlled car and showcased it at an on-campus club and career fair. This experience not only demonstrated my technical knowledge but also highlighted my leadership, problem-solving, and team management skills.

**Rube-Goldberg Machine** | Cal Poly San Luis Obispo August 2021  
Designer Arduino

- Designed 6 versions of Rube-Goldberg machine using different sensors and microcontrollers.  
- Finalized design required only a single push button interface.

**Video Game Design, Development and Production Workshop** | Cal Poly Pomona June 2021 \- July 2021  
Creator Unity3D | C\#

- Developed the video games using AI algorithms embedded in C\#.  
- Created a zombie shooting game with 3 stages of difficulty.  
- Implemented first and third person perspective, and option of switching weapons.

# Ubitus K.K.

# **Ubitus Internship Materials**

## **1\. Resume Bullet Points**

• **Engineered a semantic search MCP server** for UbiGPT LLM to query Milvus vector database with cross-collection capabilities, achieving 77.8% F1 Score through advanced re-ranking models and optimizing search performance by 95% through strategic architecture design from "Three Tools" to "One Tool" approach.

• **Architected and deployed a company-wide prompt injection defense framework** with multi-LLM architecture across 7 attack categories and 63 test rules, achieving 100% mitigation rate against injection attacks across five AI applications and enabling automated vulnerability assessment for the QA team.

• **Served as primary technical operator for G1 humanoid robot** at AI WAVE exhibition, leading live interactive demonstrations, troubleshooting technical issues, and collecting critical user interaction data for future reinforcement learning model development while supporting Marketing and BD teams.

## **2\. Overall Experience (Detailed Essay Material)**

### **Technical Projects and Evolution**

My 12-week internship at Ubitus K.K. in Taiwan was transformative both technically and philosophically. I joined the Terminal Device Group (TDG), working across web frontend, 3D, and CNF (Console and Frontier) teams on cutting-edge AI infrastructure projects.

**MCP Server Development Journey:** My primary project involved developing a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server to enable UbiGPT, the company's LLM, to perform semantic searches on their Milvus vector database. This project evolved through three distinct phases:

*Phase 1: Foundation Building* I inherited a coworker's database search algorithm in `milvus_memory.py` and transformed it into a robust MCP server. This involved containerizing the solution, establishing stable connection protocols, and standardizing data retrieval formats from dictionary lists to structured JSON strings for reliable LLM processing.

*Phase 2: Cross-Collection Architecture* The most intellectually challenging phase involved enabling searches across multiple database collections. I proposed and evaluated three distinct approaches:

* Method A: Naive approach searching all collections simultaneously (discarded due to performance concerns)  
* Method B: "Three Tools" approach providing the LLM with discrete tools (list\_collections, list\_schemas, search) for agentic discovery  
* Method C: "One Tool" approach abstracting database complexity by pre-generating collection descriptions

*Phase 3: Search Optimization* I transitioned from basic L2 Distance filtering to sophisticated re-ranking models, analyzing semantic relevance between user queries and retrieved documents. This significantly improved precision while documenting the trade-offs between speed (L2 Distance) and accuracy (Re-rank Score).

**Performance Evaluation and Data-Driven Decision Making:** I designed a comprehensive testing framework comparing methodologies across five criteria: Accuracy, Runtime, Single vs. Cross-Collection results, Cross-Collection keyword search, and Multilingual capabilities. Using F1 Score as the primary metric (balancing Precision and Recall), I proved the "One Tool" approach's superiority with faster runtime (9,194ms vs. 9,357ms) and higher accuracy (77.8% vs. 76.4% F1 Score). Runtime analysis revealed that 95% of processing time came from LLM generation rather than database operations, informing future optimization strategies.

**Security Framework Development:** My second major project involved creating a company-wide prompt injection defense framework. This comprehensive system featured:

* Multi-LLM architecture with separate target, controller, and improver LLMs  
* 63 pre-built test rules across 7 attack categories  
* Automated prompt improvement suggestions  
* Condition-based pass/fail criteria  
* YAML-based customizable rules with multiple output formats

The framework achieved 100% mitigation against injection attacks across five company AI applications, providing the QA team with automated vulnerability assessment capabilities.

**AI WAVE Exhibition Experience:** As the primary technical operator for the G1 humanoid robot at the AI WAVE exhibition, I gained invaluable experience in live technical demonstrations, real-time troubleshooting, and data collection for reinforcement learning applications. This role required quick thinking, clear communication with non-technical audiences, and seamless coordination with Marketing and BD teams.

**Technical Evolution and Learning:** Throughout the internship, I continuously improved my solutions. I migrated the MCP server from stdio to streamable-http architecture to increase calling efficiency and proposed using UV for better Python environment management company-wide. I also suggested implementing LangGraph with verification agents to critique and refine outputs, reducing errors without significant time costs.

### **Philosophical and Personal Growth**

Beyond technical achievements, this internship profoundly shaped my understanding of engineering philosophy through mentorship from Vic Chung and Melody Wang. Their guidance revealed that engineering transcends coding—it's fundamentally about problem-solving. As they taught me, “coding is essentially the description of logical processes; it serves as a tool for concretizing abstract logic.” (coding is just 描述事情的邏輯，是將邏輯具象化的工具).

Our conversations explored profound questions about life, adaptation (適者生存), the relationship between material and spiritual dimensions (物質與精神), consciousness across different dimensions, spirit, and even metaphysical concepts about God. These discussions challenged me to think beyond technical implementation toward the deeper purpose and impact of engineering work.

This experience taught me that finding the right people to solve problems is as valuable as solving them yourself, and that true engineering excellence lies in understanding systems holistically rather than just optimizing individual components.

## **3\. LinkedIn Post**

Post 1:

🚀 Just wrapped up an incredible 12-week journey as a Software Engineering Intern at [Ubitus K.K.](https://www.linkedin.com/company/ubitus/) in Taiwan\!

This experience pushed me far beyond my comfort zone and taught me that engineering is about so much more than just coding--it's about solving complex problems and thinking systematically.

🔍 Key Achievements:  
• Built a semantic search MCP server for our company LLM, achieving 77.8% F1 Score with cross-collection capabilities  
• Developed a company-wide prompt injection defense framework that blocked 100% of attacks across 5 AI applications  
• Operated the G1 humanoid robot at AI WAVE exhibition, bridging technical implementation with real-world demonstrations

🧠 Beyond the Technical:  
The most transformative part? Deep conversations with manager Vic and mentor Melody about what defines an engineer. They showed me that coding is simply "描述事情的邏輯，是將邏輯具象化的工具" \- the description of logical processes; it serves as a tool for concretizing abstract logic. Sometimes the best solution isn't writing more code, but finding the right people and approach to solve the problem.

From discussing AI consciousness to exploring philosophical questions about adaptation and existence, this internship expanded my thinking in ways I never expected. I am extremely grateful to the people I met here, especially [Ubitus \- TP Vic Chiang](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ubitus-tp-vic-chiang-5562312a8/), for your invaluable insights and creativity, and for teaching me how to tell a story and express ideas fully to listeners. A huge shoutout to my supportive mentor [Melody Wang](https://www.linkedin.com/in/melody-wang-1775a1161/) for your guidance and patience. Without [Hung-Yi Shih](https://www.linkedin.com/in/hung-yi-shih-8b4645167/)'s and [Andy Khoo Yuen Khai](https://www.linkedin.com/in/andy-khoo-yuen-khai-15a3271aa/)'s help, things couldn't have gone this well at the AI WAVE exhibition. Thank you also to [Helen Lyu](https://www.linkedin.com/in/helen-lyu/) for exposing me to the work in the Marketing and BD team.

Thank you to the entire RD/TDG/CNF team for making these 12 weeks the most memorable experience of my life. The insights and perspectives I've gained will carry forward into everything I build next.

\#Ubitus hashtag\#SoftwareEngineering hashtag\#AI hashtag\#MachineLearning hashtag\#Internship hashtag\#Taiwan hashtag\#TechCareers hashtag\#PersonalGrowth hashtag\#Milvus hashtag\#VectorDB hashtag\#RAG

Post 2:

Thrilled to share my experience representing [Ubitus K.K.](https://www.linkedin.com/company/ubitus/) at Taiwan's largest AI exhibition \-  
[hashtag](https://www.linkedin.com/search/results/all/?keywords=%23wave2025&origin=HASH_TAG_FROM_FEED)  
[\#WAVE2025](https://www.linkedin.com/search/results/all/?keywords=%23wave2025&origin=HASH_TAG_FROM_FEED)\! It was an honor to showcase our company's cutting-edge products to such an engaged audience.

I had the incredible opportunity to be at the forefront of our showcase, controlling our AI Humanoid with an Apple Vision Pro. This interactive demo wasn't just about handing out snacks; we were actively gathering valuable training data for the robot's future Reinforcement Learning models. The positive energy and interest our booth attracted were fantastic\!

We demonstrated our suite of AI solutions:  
🤖 AI Humanoid: An AI companion designed for healthcare settings like Mackey Memorial Hospital, capable of patient interaction, physical support, and specimen delivery.  
🐳 UbiOne: A dynamic platform for creating and interacting with customizable virtual characters who can chat, dance, and generate images. It supports voice interaction, multilingual communication, and emotional expression, making it perfect for VTubers, customer service, and brand characters.  
🎨 UbiArt: A powerful tool for AI-powered image and video generation with 22 style transformations. By combining natural language prompts with the Diffusion model, it can quickly generate unique illustrations and animations.  
😊 UbiReal: Our 8K hyper-real avatars. The integration of Unreal Engine and NVIDIA ACE achieves extremely realistic appearance and voice-synchronized expressions, allowing visitors to experience immersive virtual interactions.

Interacting with visitors and hearing their feedback on our products was invaluable. Exploring the expo, I observed key industry trends focused on virtual characters, diverse AI agent applications, robotics, and computer vision. It was inspiring to connect with other innovators in the field.

A huge thank you to [Amy Huang](https://www.linkedin.com/in/shihchihuang/), [Andy Khoo Yuen Khai](https://www.linkedin.com/in/andy-khoo-yuen-khai-15a3271aa/), [Helen Lyu](https://www.linkedin.com/in/helen-lyu/), [Hung-Yi Shih](https://www.linkedin.com/in/hung-yi-shih-8b4645167/). Their guidance has been instrumental in my learning journey, providing me with a holistic view of the AI industry, more specifically in the difference between Business Development, Marketing, Sales, and R\&D teams.

\#AI hashtag\#Ubitus hashtag\#Internship hashtag\#AIWAVE hashtag\#Robotics hashtag\#GenerativeAI hashtag\#VirtualCharacters hashtag\#ReinforcementLearning hashtag\#AppleVisionPro hashtag\#TechExpo

## **4\. LinkedIn Experience Description**

**Software Engineering Intern** *Ubitus K.K. · Jun 2025 \- Sep 2025 · 3 mos* *Taipei, Taiwan*

Developed advanced AI infrastructure solutions within the Terminal Device Group (TDG), focusing on semantic search optimization and security frameworks for company-wide AI applications.

**Core Achievements:** • Engineered a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server enabling UbiGPT LLM to perform cross-collection semantic searches on Milvus vector database, achieving 77.8% F1 Score through re-ranking model implementation and optimizing performance via strategic "One Tool" architecture design

• Architected and deployed a comprehensive prompt injection defense framework with multi-LLM architecture, featuring 63 test rules across 7 attack categories that achieved 100% mitigation rate against injection attacks across 5 AI applications while providing automated vulnerability assessment for QA teams

• Served as primary technical operator for G1 humanoid robot demonstrations at AI WAVE exhibition, managing live technical troubleshooting and collecting user interaction data for reinforcement learning model development

**Technical Impact:** Designed rigorous testing methodologies comparing system performance across accuracy, runtime, cross-collection capabilities, and multilingual support. Conducted comprehensive performance analysis revealing that 95% of processing time originated from LLM generation rather than database operations, informing future optimization strategies.

**Skills Developed:** Python, RAG, VectorDB, LangGraph, Milvus, Docker, MCP, Prompt Engineering, System Architecture, Performance Optimization, Multilingual AI Systems
